note: Author-side of the review loop: fetch UNRESOLVED PR threads, apply minimal fixes, push, reply + resolve. Closest siblings: task-fix-pr (autonomous same-contract variant), pr-review (reviewer side), pr-create, pr-merge, commit. mock_rate encodes the IDEAL outcome for --decider mock; real deciders ignore it.

queries.text: resolve the PR
address the review comments on my PR and push the fixes
reviewer left a bunch of comments on #128 — apply them and mark the threads resolved
can you work through the unresolved review threads on my PR? fix each one and reply
my teammate requested changes on the PR, go apply the feedback and close out the threads
update the PR based on the review — the null-check comment and the rename they asked for
marvin resolve PR 42, then reply to the reviewers with the commit sha
there are open review comments on my pull request, deal with them and resolve the threads
apply the PR feedback for the auth branch and push, some are nits some are real changes
the reviewer approved with a few requested changes — knock those out and mark them done
review my PR and leave inline comments on the diff
queries.note: reviewer side → pr-review, not resolving feedback

queries.text: merge PR #42 and switch back to dev
open a pull request for my feature branch
there's a merge conflict in src/api/handler.ts between my branch and dev, help me resolve it
queries.note: 'resolve' means conflict resolution, a plain git op — not review feedback

queries.text: the reviewer's comment says this query is an N+1 but I don't get why — explain what they mean
queries.note: understanding a comment → explain, no code change requested

queries.text: reviewer flagged a race condition in the worker pool — help me figure out if it's real
queries.note: root-cause investigation → debug, not applying feedback

queries.text: commit the changes I already made from the review
queries.note: just committing existing edits → commit; no thread fetch/reply/resolve

queries.text: reply to the reviewer that this contradicts the spec and I won't change it
queries.note: a single manual GitHub reply, not the full apply-push-resolve cycle

queries.text: close PR #99 without merging, we're abandoning that approach
queries.note: plain gh pr close, not resolving feedback

queries.text: resolve which commit introduced the failing test on the PR branch
review comments came in on my PR — apply them all inline, reply to each thread, and resolve them
queries.note: shares 'review comments' vocab with pr-review but the action is the author applying + resolving feedback interactively

queries.text: resolve the review feedback on PR #55 right here, walk me through each change before you apply it
queries.note: interactive inline path beats task-fix-pr's autonomous one-shot dispatch

queries.text: dispatch an agent to autonomously address all the PR review comments end-to-end and report back
queries.note: addressing PR review comments is pr-resolve's core job (headless task-fix-pr is not auto-invocable)

queries.text: review the pull request and post your feedback as inline GitHub comments
queries.note: reviewer side (leaving comments) is pr-review; pr-resolve clears them
